title: Global Packages and Publishing
description: Install packages globally and share your own packages.

slides.title: Global Installation 🌍
slides.content:
# Global vs Local Packages

Most packages are installed **locally** (inside `node_modules` of your project). Some packages are meant to be used as command-line tools and are better installed **globally**.

Install globally with `-g` flag:
```bash
npm install -g nodemon
```

Global packages are installed in a system directory (e.g., `/usr/local/lib/node_modules`) and their executables are added to your PATH.

Common globally used packages:
- `nodemon` – auto-restart server on changes
- `http-server` – simple static server
- `create-react-app` – bootstrap React apps

List global packages:
```bash
npm list -g --depth=0
```

slides.title: ⚠️ Permission Issues with Global Installs
slides.content:
## Avoid sudo with npm

On macOS/Linux, installing globally may require `sudo` if npm is installed in a system directory. This is risky and can lead to permission problems.

**Better solution:** Fix npm's permissions by changing the global directory to your home folder.

```bash
mkdir ~/.npm-global
npm config set prefix '~/.npm-global'
```
Then add `~/.npm-global/bin` to your PATH.

Alternatively, use a version manager like `nvm` – it manages Node installations in your home directory, so no `sudo` needed.

slides.title: Publishing Your Own Package 📤
slides.content:
## Share Your Code with the World

You can publish your own npm package! Steps:

1. Create an account on [npmjs.com](https://npmjs.com).
2. Log in via CLI: `npm login`
3. Prepare your package:
   - Ensure `package.json` has `name`, `version`, `main` entry.
   - Make sure the name is unique (search npm registry).
4. Publish: `npm publish`

After publishing, anyone can install it with `npm install your-package-name`.

You can update versions by changing `version` in package.json (following SemVer) and publishing again.
